title: fix(gen): throttle image server registry KV writes (1×/30s per key)
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
## Summary
- Throttle \`registerServer\` KV writes to once-per-30s per key (in-memory Map, per-isolate)
- Eliminates ~100 daily \`KV PUT failed: 429 Too Many Requests\` errors on \`POST /register\` caused by GPU servers heartbeat-registering faster than Cloudflare KV's 1-write/sec/key cap

## Why this is safe
- Previous write already sets \`expirationTtl=240s\`, so the registry entry stays fresh well past the 30s throttle
- Per-isolate Map → different isolates each get one write; bounded by KV key count (a few dozen image GPU servers)
- Throttled writes are logged but don't error out

## Test plan
- [x] \`npx tsc --noEmit\` clean
- [x] \`npx vitest run\` (220/220 pass)

gist.headline: Calming the heartbeat
gist.blurb: We've smoothed out how our GPU servers check in with the hive, eliminating those pesky 429 errors from Cloudflare KV.
gist.summary: Throttled image server registry KV writes to once per 30 seconds per key to prevent Cloudflare KV 429 rate limit errors during GPU server heartbeats.
gist.impact: This eliminates roughly 100 daily failed registration errors, improving the stability of our internal server tracking without affecting server availability.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee acting as a gentle traffic controller for a busy server rack, slowing down the rapid 'heartbeats' to a manageable pace. The monitor robot looks relieved.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny traffic controller hat, gently holding up a stopwatch next to a glowing retro server rack. A chunky robot with a monitor head looks relieved as the server's blinking lights slow down to a calm rhythm. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
